summary:
EXECUTIVE READ
India activity concentrates in colocation (Colo) with strong signals in Maharashtra, Telangana, Uttar Pradesh, and Tamil Nadu, driven by state-level data centre policies and hyperscaler expansion. The top India policy hook is the UP cabinet-approved Data Centre Policy 2026 targeting Rs 2 lakh crore investment, alongside Gujarat’s 2026-29 policy. Value-chain movers include Colo (dominated by Indian operators like Sify and Yotta) and Build layers, with foreign hyperscalers like Amazon, Google, Meta, and AWS actively scaling via land leases, job postings, and facility investments. This run’s foreign-hyperscaler India moves are led by Amazon’s Rs 650-crore Mumbai land lease and Meta’s $13 billion Jamnagar project with Reliance, signaling aggressive infrastructure buildout.

STATE PLAYS  🤖AI-draft:
The current prospect slate should be steered toward Maharashtra (P1) for its mature Mumbai/Navi Mumbai ecosystem and strong legal/economic policy detail, Uttar Pradesh (P1) for aggressive NCR/inland cluster incentives targeting AI-ready scale (pending official GO/PDF for clause-level use), and Telangana (P1) for operator-friendly Hyderabad AI/cloud regime with essential-service treatment. Upside states include Odisha (P2) for generous white-space policy for first movers, despite medium-low execution, and Gujarat (P2) for Dholera/GIFT green AI-ready west-coast challenger with official capex/tariff support, though demand depth and MoU detail require validation. Tamil Nadu’s policy expired March 31, 2026, so renewal must be confirmed before pitching incentives there.
